name: Getimg.ai
what_it_does: Unified REST API for AI image generation supporting 60+ models including Stable Diffusion, FLUX.1, and fine-tuned variants, covering text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, outpainting, upscaling, and ControlNet workflows.
use_cases: High-volume automated image generation for content pipelines where agents create product images, illustrations, or marketing assets
Agent-driven creative workflows combining text-to-image generation with inpainting for iterative image refinement
Building image generation features into applications without managing GPU infrastructure or model weights
Generating training data or synthetic images at scale using multiple model styles via a single API endpoint
not_for: Use cases requiring real-time image generation with sub-second latency (generation takes 1-5 seconds minimum)
Applications needing NSFW content generation on standard plans — restricted by default
Teams requiring on-premise or private cloud deployment for data sovereignty of generated images
best_when: Best when an agent needs to generate images at moderate-to-high volume using diverse model styles without managing GPU infrastructure, and a simple API key + REST interface is sufficient.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need generation latency under 1 second, require very large image outputs (>2048px) at high volume, or need fine-grained control over model weights and sampling parameters beyond what the API exposes.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Keys are account-scoped with no permission granularity. Simple and agent-friendly — no OAuth flow or token refresh required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $10 in free trial credits upon signup. Credits do not expire.
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic plan from $12/month for approximately 1,000 standard image generations. Starter, Hobby, and Pro plans scale up in credit volume. Enterprise plans available with volume discounts.
pricing.notes: Pricing is credit-based. Credit cost per image varies by model (FLUX.1 costs more credits than SD 1.5) and by resolution. SDXL images cost more credits than standard 512px generations. Credits can also be purchased in bundles without a subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan. Basic plans allow approximately 10 concurrent requests. Higher plans allow more concurrency. Specific TPS limits not publicly documented.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON error responses with error codes and human-readable messages. Prompt content policy violations return clear 4xx errors. Credit exhaustion returns a specific error code. Generally good error clarity for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support — each API call consumes credits and generates a new image. Agents should implement their own result caching to avoid re-generating identical prompts.
known_agent_gotchas: Each API call deducts credits regardless of whether the generated image is actually used — agents must implement result caching
Model availability changes over time as models are added or deprecated; agents should not hardcode model IDs without version pinning
ControlNet workflows require separate preprocessing steps (depth map, edge detection) before the main generation call
Image URLs returned in responses are temporary and expire — agents must download and store images immediately
Content policy enforcement is model-dependent; same prompt may succeed on one model and fail on another
No batch endpoint — high-volume agents must manage concurrent requests within rate limits

security_score_components.security_notes: Simple API key auth with no scope granularity means a compromised key grants full account access. Generated images are stored temporarily on Getimg.ai CDN — operators should download and delete promptly if sensitive. No data processing agreements publicly available.
